question: [File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / HashLib.hashOffer] Can an unprivileged taker construct a ratifierData with a valid Merkle proof for an offer where offer.maxAssets=0 and offer.maxUnits=0 (both zero), bypassing the atMostOneNonZero check in take (which requires at most one nonzero, not at least one nonzero), so that consumed[maker][group] += 0 every fill, allowing the offer to be filled unlimited times? Preconditions: maker signs an offer with maxAssets=0 and maxUnits=0. Call sequence: isRatified (passes) -> take -> atMostOneNonZero(0, 0) = true -> consumed += 0 -> require(0 <= 0) passes. Invariant tested: offers with both maxAssets=0 and maxUnits=0 must not be fillable unlimited times. Scoped impact: offer replay - unlimited fills of a zero-cap offer. Proof idea: unit test with maxAssets=maxUnits=0, fill N times, assert consumed remains 0 and N fills succeed.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the continuous fee accrual formula buyerPendingFeeIncrease = mulDivDown(buyerCreditIncrease * continuousFee * timeToMaturity, WAD) where continuousFee is in units of WAD/second and timeToMaturity is in seconds, so that for large timeToMaturity (near 100 years = ~3.15e9 seconds) and large buyerCreditIncrease (near type(uint128).max), the product buyerCreditIncrease * continuousFee * timeToMaturity overflows uint256, causing an incorrect (small) pendingFee? Preconditions: market with maturity = block.timestamp + 100 years, continuousFee = MAX_CONTINUOUS_FEE, buyerCreditIncrease near type(uint128).max. Call sequence: isRatified -> take -> mulDivDown(buyerCreditIncrease * continuousFee * timeToMaturity, WAD) overflows. Invariant tested: pendingFee must be correctly computed without overflow. Scoped impact: systematic continuous fee leakage via overflow. Proof idea: unit test with max parameters, assert no overflow in pendingFee computation.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker, by executing a maker's signed buy offer at TTM just below 360 days (e.g., 359 days 23 hours), cause the settlement fee to be computed via interpolation between cbp5 and cbp6 rather than using cbp6 directly (which applies at TTM >= 360 days), potentially resulting in a different fee than the maker expected when signing, and if the interpolated fee is lower, the taker pays less than the maker intended? Preconditions: offer.buy=true, TTM = 360 days - 1 second. Call sequence: isRatified -> take -> settlementFee uses (180d, 360d, cbp5, cbp6) interpolation. Invariant tested: settlement fee must be consistent with maker's price expectation at signing. Scoped impact: extractable value via fee boundary discontinuity. Proof idea: assert settlementFee(id, 360 days - 1) == settlementFee(id, 360 days) or document the difference.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the fact that for a buy offer, buyerAssets = mulDivDown(units, buyerPrice, WAD) and sellerAssets = mulDivDown(units, sellerPrice, WAD), so that the fee collected is mulDivDown(units, buyerPrice, WAD) - mulDivDown(units, sellerPrice, WAD), which may differ from mulDivDown(units, buyerPrice - sellerPrice, WAD) = mulDivDown(units, settlementFee, WAD) by up to 1 wei per fill, causing systematic fee accounting drift over many fills? Preconditions: offer.buy=true, many fills. Call sequence: isRatified -> take (N times) -> claimableSettlementFee accumulates. Invariant tested: sum(buyerAssets_i - sellerAssets_i) must equal sum(mulDivDown(units_i, settlementFee, WAD)) within 1 wei per fill. Scoped impact: systematic fee accounting drift. Proof idea: invariant test asserting claimableSettlementFee == sum of expected fees within N wei tolerance.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the interaction between the offer's group-based consumption tracking and the TTM-dependent fee, where two offers in the same group have different ticks but the same maxAssets, so that filling the lower-tick offer (lower price, lower buyerAssets per unit) consumes less of the group budget per unit, allowing more total units to be filled across the group than the maker intended when setting maxAssets? Preconditions: two offers in same group, different ticks, offer.buy=true, offer.maxAssets=X. Call sequence: isRatified(offer1) -> take (fills offer1 at low price, low consumed per unit); isRatified(offer2) -> take (fills offer2). Invariant tested: group budget must cap total economic exposure regardless of which offer is filled. Scoped impact: offer overfill via group budget exploitation. Proof idea: unit test with two offers in same group, assert total units filled * avg_price <= maxAssets.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the fact that the settlement fee interpolation formula (feeLower*(end-TTM) + feeUpper*(TTM-start)) / (end-start) uses integer division that truncates, so that for TTM values where the numerator is not divisible by (end-start), the fee is rounded down, and the taker can choose TTM values (by timing the transaction) that maximize the rounding discount, systematically paying less fee than the protocol intends? Preconditions: market with non-zero settlement fee cbps; taker can choose block.timestamp within a range. Call sequence: isRatified -> take at chosen TTM -> settlementFee rounds down. Invariant tested: settlement fee must not be manipulable by taker's choice of execution timestamp. Scoped impact: systematic protocol fee leakage via timestamp selection. Proof idea: fuzz TTM in [0, 360 days] and compute max rounding discount per fill.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker, for a sell offer (offer.buy=false) with maxUnits-based consumption, exploit the fact that consumed[maker][group] += units (exact) while the taker pays buyerAssets = mulDivUp(units, buyerPrice, WAD) (rounds up), so that the taker overpays by up to 1 wei per fill, and across many fills the taker loses value that goes to the maker, creating a systematic value transfer from taker to maker that the taker cannot avoid? Preconditions: offer.buy=false, offer.maxUnits>0, buyerPrice not divisor of WAD. Call sequence: isRatified -> take (N times) -> buyerAssets = mulDivUp(units, buyerPrice, WAD). Invariant tested: taker must not systematically overpay due to rounding. Scoped impact: systematic value leakage from taker to maker. Proof idea: fuzz N fills and assert total_buyerAssets <= N * ceil(units * buyerPrice / WAD).

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the fact that the offer hash commits to offer.market (including maturity) but the settlement fee is computed from block.timestamp at execution, so that a maker who signs an offer for a market with maturity=T intending a specific fee schedule finds that if the market's fee breakpoints are changed by the feeSetter between signing and execution, the offer is filled at a completely different fee, potentially making the offer economically worthless or harmful to the maker? Preconditions: maker signs offer; feeSetter changes all cbps to maximum; taker executes. Call sequence: isRatified (passes) -> take (uses new max fee) -> sellerPrice = offerPrice - maxFee potentially negative. Invariant tested: offer execution must not revert or produce negative prices due to fee changes. Scoped impact: fund freeze or value leakage via fee schedule manipulation. Proof idea: unit test: sign offer at tick=0 (price near 1), set fee to max, execute, assert sellerPrice >= 0.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the interaction between the offer's tick-based price and the settlement fee, where for a buy offer at tick=MAX_TICK (lowest price near 0), the settlement fee could exceed offerPrice, causing sellerPrice = offerPrice - settlementFee to underflow (since both are uint256 and the subtraction is unchecked in take), resulting in a very large sellerPrice and incorrect asset calculations? Preconditions: offer.buy=true, tick=MAX_TICK (price near 0), settlementFee > offerPrice. Call sequence: isRatified -> take -> sellerPrice = offerPrice - settlementFee (underflow). Invariant tested: sellerPrice must never underflow. Scoped impact: accounting corruption via underflow. Proof idea: unit test with tick=MAX_TICK and max settlement fee, assert take reverts or sellerPrice >= 0.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the fact that for a buy offer, the consumed amount is buyerAssets = mulDivDown(units, buyerPrice, WAD), and buyerPrice = sellerPrice + settlementFee where settlementFee changes with TTM, so that by executing at a TTM where the fee is at a local minimum (e.g., TTM=0 post-maturity with cbp0=0), the taker maximizes units per unit of consumed budget, effectively getting more units than the maker intended for the same maxAssets? Preconditions: offer.buy=true, offer.maxAssets>0, cbp0=0 (zero post-maturity fee). Call sequence: isRatified -> take post-maturity -> buyerPrice = offerPrice (no fee) -> consumed += mulDivDown(units, offerPrice, WAD). Invariant tested: maxAssets must cap total units filled regardless of fee level. Scoped impact: offer overfill via zero-fee post-maturity execution. Proof idea: compare total units filled at TTM=360d vs TTM=0 for same maxAssets.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the fact that the offer hash includes offer.maxAssets but the actual assets per unit depend on both tick and TTM-dependent fee, so that a maker who sets maxAssets to cap their total loan token exposure finds that at different TTMs the same maxAssets allows different total units, and by executing at the TTM that maximizes units per asset, the taker can extract more units from the maker than intended? Preconditions: offer.buy=true, offer.maxAssets=X, taker waits for TTM where fee is minimized. Call sequence: isRatified -> take at low-fee TTM -> consumed += mulDivDown(units, buyerPrice_low_fee, WAD). Invariant tested: maker's economic exposure must be bounded by maxAssets regardless of TTM. Scoped impact: extractable value via TTM-optimized fill timing. Proof idea: compute max units fillable at TTM=0 vs TTM=360d for same maxAssets and tick.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the fact that the EcrecoverRatifier checks isAuthorized[offer.maker][offer.ratifier] in Midnight.take (line 355) before calling isRatified, and isRatified also checks isAuthorized[offer.maker][_signer], so that if the maker authorizes the ratifier contract itself as a signer, the ratifier could ratify offers signed by itself (address(this)), creating a self-referential authorization loop that bypasses the maker's intent? Preconditions: maker authorizes EcrecoverRatifier address as both ratifier and signer. Call sequence: setIsAuthorized(EcrecoverRatifier, true, maker) -> take -> isRatified checks _signer == EcrecoverRatifier -> isAuthorized[maker][EcrecoverRatifier] = true -> passes. Invariant tested: ratifier authorization must not allow the ratifier to self-authorize offers. Scoped impact: gate bypass - unauthorized offer ratification. Proof idea: unit test where EcrecoverRatifier is both ratifier and authorized signer.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the fact that the settlement fee is a piecewise linear function with 7 breakpoints stored as uint16 cbp values (each cbp = 1e12 WAD), so that the maximum representable fee per breakpoint is 65535 * 1e12 = 6.5535e16 WAD (about 6.5%), but the protocol cap is 50 bps = 0.5%, meaning the fee can never cause sellerPrice underflow for any valid tick, but the rounding in cbp storage (fee must be multiple of CBP=1e12) creates a quantization error of up to 1e12 per breakpoint that accumulates in the interpolation? Preconditions: market with fee set to maximum allowed cbp values. Call sequence: isRatified -> take -> settlementFee computed with cbp quantization. Invariant tested: settlement fee quantization must not create exploitable rounding errors. Scoped impact: systematic fee accounting error due to cbp quantization. Proof idea: assert settlementFee(id, TTM) is within CBP of the true continuous fee for all TTM.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the interaction between the offer's group consumption tracking and mulDivDown rounding, where for a buy offer with maxAssets=X, each fill consumes mulDivDown(units, buyerPrice, WAD) which rounds down, so that the last fill can push consumed to exactly maxAssets while the actual units transferred slightly exceed what maxAssets / buyerPrice should allow, extracting a fractional unit of value? Preconditions: offer.buy=true, offer.maxAssets=X, final fill chosen so consumed + mulDivDown(units, buyerPrice, WAD) == maxAssets exactly. Call sequence: isRatified -> take (N-1 fills) -> take (final fill with units chosen to hit maxAssets exactly). Invariant tested: total units filled must not exceed floor(maxAssets * WAD / buyerPrice). Scoped impact: extractable value via boundary fill exploitation. Proof idea: unit test with maxAssets=1000, buyerPrice=333, fill to boundary and assert total units.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the fact that the offer hash commits to offer.market.maturity but the continuous fee accrual in buyerPendingFeeIncrease uses timeToMaturity = zeroFloorSub(maturity, block.timestamp), so that two offers with the same tick but different maturities (and thus different TTMs at the same block.timestamp) produce different pendingFee obligations for the same units, and a taker can choose the offer with the shorter maturity to minimize their continuous fee obligation? Preconditions: two markets with same tick but different maturities; taker chooses shorter maturity. Call sequence: isRatified(offer_short_maturity) -> take -> pendingFee = mulDivDown(units * continuousFee * short_TTM, WAD). Invariant tested: continuous fee must be proportional to TTM and not avoidable by market selection. Scoped impact: systematic continuous fee leakage via maturity selection. Proof idea: compare pendingFee for same units at TTM=1 day vs TTM=360 days.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the fact that for a sell offer (offer.buy=false), the taker is the buyer and pays buyerAssets = mulDivUp(units, buyerPrice, WAD), while the maker (seller) receives sellerAssets = mulDivUp(units, sellerPrice, WAD), and the fee is buyerAssets - sellerAssets, so that if both round up by 1 the fee is 0 even when settlementFee > 0, and the protocol collects no fee for this trade? Preconditions: offer.buy=false, units chosen so units * buyerPrice % WAD != 0 and units * sellerPrice % WAD != 0 but both round up to the same value. Call sequence: isRatified -> take -> claimableSettlementFee += mulDivUp(units, buyerPrice, WAD) - mulDivUp(units, sellerPrice, WAD). Invariant tested: fee collected must be positive whenever settlementFee > 0 and units > 0. Scoped impact: systematic protocol fee leakage. Proof idea: fuzz (units, tick, TTM) for sell offers and assert buyerAssets > sellerAssets whenever settlementFee > 0.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the fact that the offer hash includes offer.tick but not the tick spacing, so that if the tick spacing setter decreases the market's tick spacing after the maker signs the offer, new ticks become accessible, but the maker's signed offer at the old tick is still valid, and the taker can fill it at the same tick but now the tick is at a finer granularity, potentially changing the economic meaning of the offer? Preconditions: market with DEFAULT_TICK_SPACING=4; tickSpacingSetter decreases to 1; maker's offer at tick=4 is still valid. Call sequence: setMarketTickSpacing(id, 1) -> isRatified(offer at tick=4) -> take. Invariant tested: tick spacing change must not invalidate or alter the economics of existing signed offers. Scoped impact: offer economics altered by tick spacing change. Proof idea: unit test: sign offer at tick=4, decrease spacing to 1, execute, assert economics unchanged.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the fact that the settlement fee at TTM=0 (post-maturity) is exactly settlementFeeCbp0 * CBP (the 0-day breakpoint), and if cbp0=0 (zero fee at maturity), then buyerPrice = sellerPrice = offerPrice, so that buyerAssets = sellerAssets and the protocol collects zero fee, while the taker still acquires credit at the offer price, potentially allowing the taker to arbitrage between the offer price and the post-maturity redemption value? Preconditions: market post-maturity, cbp0=0, offer.buy=true. Call sequence: isRatified -> take post-maturity -> settlementFee=0 -> buyerAssets=sellerAssets -> fee=0. Invariant tested: protocol must collect settlement fee even post-maturity if cbp0 > 0. Scoped impact: systematic fee leakage if cbp0=0 is set. Proof idea: unit test post-maturity with cbp0=0, assert fee collected is 0 and quantify.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the interaction between the offer's maxAssets cap and the settlement fee, where for a buy offer the consumed amount is buyerAssets (which includes the fee), so that a maker who sets maxAssets=X to cap their credit exposure actually caps their total outflow (credit + fee), meaning their effective credit exposure is maxAssets - totalFees, which is less than X, and the taker can exploit this by choosing TTMs with higher fees to reduce the maker's effective credit capacity? Preconditions: offer.buy=true, offer.maxAssets=X, taker executes at high-fee TTM. Call sequence: isRatified -> take at high TTM -> consumed += buyerAssets (includes fee). Invariant tested: maker's credit exposure must equal maxAssets minus fees, not maxAssets. Scoped impact: systematic reduction of maker's effective lending capacity. Proof idea: compare total credit units filled at TTM=0 vs TTM=360d for same maxAssets.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / HashLib.hashOffer] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the fact that HashLib.hashOffer includes keccak256(offer.callbackData) rather than offer.callbackData directly, so that two offers with different callbackData that hash to the same keccak256 (a collision) would produce the same offer hash, allowing a taker to substitute different callbackData while passing the Merkle proof, potentially triggering unintended callback behavior? Preconditions: keccak256 collision in callbackData (theoretical). Call sequence: isRatified(offer with callbackData_A) passes; taker substitutes callbackData_B with same hash. Invariant tested: offer hash must uniquely identify all offer parameters including callbackData. Scoped impact: callback confusion via hash collision. Proof idea: note this requires keccak256 collision; document as theoretical and test that callbackData is correctly bound.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the fact that the settlement fee breakpoints are stored as uint16 cbp values (max 65535 cbp = 65535e12 WAD), but the maximum allowed fee is 50 bps = 5e15 WAD = 5000 cbp, so that the cbp storage has 60535 unused values, and if a future fee setter sets a fee above the current max (due to a bug in maxSettlementFee validation), the interpolation could produce fees that cause sellerPrice underflow? Preconditions: feeSetter sets cbp to value above 5000 (hypothetical bug). Call sequence: isRatified -> take -> settlementFee > offerPrice -> sellerPrice underflows. Invariant tested: settlement fee must never exceed offerPrice for any valid tick. Scoped impact: accounting corruption via fee exceeding price. Proof idea: assert for all valid (tick, cbp) combinations that settlementFee(id, TTM) <= tickToPrice(tick).

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the fact that the offer hash commits to offer.market which includes the full collateralParams array (with lltv, maxLif, oracle), but the settlement fee depends only on the market ID and TTM, so that two markets with identical parameters except for collateralParams (different LLTV tiers) have different IDs but potentially the same settlement fee schedule, and a taker can fill an offer intended for one market in the other if the maker accidentally signs for the wrong market? Preconditions: maker signs offer for market A; taker submits it to market B with same fee schedule. Call sequence: isRatified(offer_A) -> take in market B -> hashOffer includes market, so offer_A hash != offer_B hash -> take reverts. Invariant tested: offer must be bound to its specific market. Scoped impact: cross-market offer replay. Proof idea: unit test submitting offer for market A to market B, assert take reverts.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the rounding in the continuous fee accrual formula buyerPendingFeeIncrease = mulDivDown(buyerCreditIncrease * continuousFee * timeToMaturity, WAD) where the product is divided by WAD (1e18), so that for small buyerCreditIncrease (e.g., 1 unit) and small continuousFee, the result is 0, meaning the lender acquires credit with zero pending fee, and by splitting a large fill into many small fills of 1 unit each, the taker can minimize the total pendingFee charged? Preconditions: offer.buy=true, continuousFee > 0 but small, taker splits fill into N=1-unit fills. Call sequence: isRatified -> take(units=1) repeated N times -> pendingFee = 0 each time. Invariant tested: total pendingFee must equal N * mulDivDown(1, continuousFee * TTM, WAD) which may be 0. Scoped impact: systematic continuous fee leakage via fill splitting. Proof idea: compare pendingFee for 1 fill of N units vs N fills of 1 unit.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the fact that the offer hash includes offer.market.maturity as a uint256, and the settlement fee computation uses timeToMaturity = zeroFloorSub(maturity, block.timestamp), so that for two offers with maturities T and T+1 (differing by 1 second), the settlement fees at the same block.timestamp differ by at most (feeUpper - feeLower) / (end - start) per second, and by choosing the offer with the maturity that maximizes TTM (and thus minimizes fee in the decreasing-fee region), the taker can minimize the fee paid? Preconditions: two offers with maturities T and T+1, same tick. Call sequence: isRatified(offer_T+1) -> take -> lower fee due to higher TTM. Invariant tested: fee minimization via maturity selection must be bounded. Scoped impact: systematic fee leakage via maturity selection. Proof idea: compute fee difference for adjacent maturities and assert it is bounded by CBP.
